body.title: Day-close stamps next-day slate with completion marker
body.result: stale_slate_check.py extended to detect completion marker; day-close step 13.8 writes it; context-load docs updated
body.description:
After compaction, the next session can't reliably detect that day-close already ran for yesterday. The current session re-ran the 2026-05-18 day-close because there was no explicit signal it had completed.

Day-close already creates today's slate in step 1, but nothing stamps a durable completion signal into that slate. Add a final step that writes:

  ## Day-close for YYYY-MM-DD: complete HH:MM UTC

to the new day's slate (the one created in step 1). Context-load step 0.25 (stale-slate check) already reads the prior day's slate — extend it to also scan today's slate for this marker and emit 'day-close for yesterday: already complete' when found, suppressing the re-run offer.

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/skills/day-close (add final stamp step), ~/.claude/skills/context-load (extend stale-slate check to read today's slate for the marker)
**Design rules:** none apply — flat-file write only, no DB
**Scope boundary:** IN: write the marker, read it in context-load. OUT: backfilling past slates, changing the audit or any other day-close steps.
**Test plan:** Run day-close, verify today's slate contains the marker line. In a fresh session, verify context-load reports 'day-close already complete' without offering to re-run.
